topics.title: AI Infrastructure
topics.executive_summary: <cite index="3-27">NVIDIA signaled a busy second half of 2026 with Grace Blackwell, Vera Rubin, and a surprise unannounced product</cite>, while <cite index="5-5,5-6">NVIDIA and Microsoft plan to reinvent the PC with new AI-focused system-on-chips that sent AMD, Intel and Qualcomm shares lower</cite>.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPU hits production, deploys to major clouds
stories.body: <cite index="4-4,4-5">NVIDIA Rubin is in full production with Rubin-based products available from partners in the second half of 2026, with AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft and OCI among early deployers</cite>. <cite index="2-7">The Vera Rubin NVL72 delivers 10x more throughput per watt and 1/10th the token cost with one-fourth the GPUs for training</cite>.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Vera CPU and networking silicon enter production
stories.body: <cite index="3-31">NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics—the first 200Gb/s SerDes Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics for million-GPU factories—is now in production with CoreWeave, Lambda and Oracle among early adopters</cite>. <cite index="3-32">NVIDIA Vera CPU delivers 88 cores and 3.6TB/s on-chip fabric for agent workloads</cite>.

stories.headline: Intel positions itself as full-stack AI infrastructure provider
stories.body: <cite index="8-1,8-5,8-6">At Computex 2026, Intel transformed itself into a full-stack AI story from Xeon 6+ data center chips to edge platforms, pitching rackscale AI systems built around Xeon CPUs and SambaNova RDUs</cite>. <cite index="8-18">Intel's Crescent Island AI inference GPU using cheaper LPDDR5x and air cooling is set for limited shipments by late 2026</cite>.

stories.headline: NVIDIA enters PC market with RTX Spark, targets ARM SoC dominance
stories.body: <cite index="5-5,5-9">NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang said his company and Microsoft will "reinvent the PC", with NVIDIA unveiling a powerful laptop chip for Windows machines on June 1</cite>. <cite index="5-1,5-6">The plan to build system-on-chips for PCs sent shares of AMD, Intel and Qualcomm downward, signaling NVIDIA's push beyond data centers to edge devices</cite>.
